text: Entering this modern, slightly sterile space, I sense anticipation and slight unease. The clean lines and the glass feel like boundaries both literal and symbolic—transparent but enclosing. As I get closer to the exhibit with "R+D" boldly displayed, my mind is drawn to the interplay between visibility and interpretation. Surrounded by messages about research and coexistence, I feel simultaneously observed and isolated, as though my presence here is measured and contextualized in ways not fully within my control. The words on the screen echo my internal state—self-conscious, reflective, wondering what it means to be seen or misunderstood.
